This project demonstrates the execution of a hardware-based HID (Human Interface Device) attack, establishing a reverse shell, and exploring post-exploitation persistence. By simulating a real-world attack vector using a Raspberry Pi Pico, this exercise highlights the importance of physical security, endpoint protection, and network monitoring.
